chapter_title.ar: باب مِقْدَارِ الْمَاءِ الَّذِي يُجْزِئُ لِلْوُضُوءِ وَ الْغُسْلِ وَ مَنْ تَعَدَّى فِي الْوُضُوءِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 14 – Measurement of the water which suffices for the Ablution (Wudhu) and the washing, the one who exceeds in the Ablution (Wudhu)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/67)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/67)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ إِنَّ أَبِي كَانَ يَقُولُ إِنَّ لِلْوُضُوءِ حَدّاً مَنْ تَعَدَّاهُ لَمْ يُؤْجَرْ وَ كَانَ أَبِي يَقُولُ إِنَّمَا يَتَلَدَّدُ فَقَالَ لَهُ رَجُلٌ وَ مَا حَدُّهُ قَالَ تَغْسِلُ وَجْهَكَ وَ يَدَيْكَ وَ تَمْسَحُ رَأْسَكَ وَ رِجْلَيْكَ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, and Abu Dawood, altogether from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Fazalat, from Dawood Bin Farqad who said,

‘I heard Abu Abdullahasws saying: ‘Myasws fatherasws was saying that for the Ablution (Wudhu) there is a Limit. The one who exceeds it would not be Recompensed; and myasws fatherasws was saying: ‘But rather, he would be bewildered’. So a man said to himasws, ‘And what is its Limit?’ Heasws said: ‘You should wash your face, and your hands, and wipe your head and your feet’.
en.sarwar: 3. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad and abu Dawud all from al-Husayn ibn Sa‘id from Fadalah ibn Dawud ibn Farqad who has said the following: “I once heard abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, say, ‘My father would say, “Wudu 5 has certain limits. If one trespasses such limits, he will not receive any reward.” My father would also say, “(Certain people) only make it perplexed.’” A man then asked, ‘What then are the limits of Wudu’?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘Wash your face and hands and wipe your head and feet.’”
